title: quercus rotundifolia f. expansa
intro: Quercus rotundifolia f. expansa is a variant of the holm oak tree, a species of evergreen tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus rotundifolia f. expansa are thought to include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, with some traditional preparations also using the leaves to make a decoction or tincture.
